prompts: The golden ratio, represented by the Greek letter φ (phi), is approximately 1.618 and appears in geometry when a line is divided into two segments such that the whole line's length divided by the longer segment is equal to the longer segment divided by the shorter segment. This ratio is also present in the Fibonacci sequence, where the ratio of consecutive Fibonacci numbers approaches the golden ratio as the numbers increase. In geometric terms, the golden ratio often manifests in the form of a golden rectangle, where the ratio of the longer side to the shorter side is φ.
